title: Codex users push for deterministic sensitive-file exclusion
friendlyTitle: Codex users want a safer way to hide secret files
summary: A heavily discussed OpenAI Codex issue asks for repo-level and global ignore rules, such as `.codexignore` or a tool-neutral `.agentignore`, to prevent agents from reading or sending secrets and sensitive files to model context.
friendlySummary: People are asking OpenAI Codex for a clear rule file that tells the agent which files it must never read, such as `.env`, private keys, and cloud credentials.
whyItMatters: As coding agents move into real repositories, secret handling is becoming a workflow requirement, not a nice-to-have. Teams using AI coding tools should treat model-context access as part of their security boundary and define explicit deny rules where possible.
friendlyWhyItMatters: This matters because AI coding tools can accidentally see sensitive files. A simple blocklist would make them safer for real work projects.
